CLIPLoader.name: CLIPLoader
CLIPLoader.description:
[Recipes]

stable_diffusion: clip-l
stable_cascade: clip-g
sd3: t5 xxl/ clip-g / clip-l
stable_audio: t5 base
mochi: t5 xxl
cogvideox: t5 xxl (226-token padding)
cosmos: old t5 xxl
lumina2: gemma 2 2B
wan: umt5 xxl
 hidream: llama-3.1 (Recommend) or t5
omnigen2: qwen vl 2.5 3B
lens: gpt-oss-20b
 pixeldit: gemma 2 2B elm

DualCLIPLoader.name: DualCLIPLoader
DualCLIPLoader.description:
[Recipes]

sdxl: clip-l, clip-g
sd3: clip-l, clip-g / clip-l, t5 / clip-g, t5
flux: clip-l, t5
hidream: at least one of t5 or llama, recommended t5 and llama
hunyuan_image: qwen2.5vl 7b and byt5 small
newbie: gemma-3-4b-it, jina clip v2

destination.tooltip: The destination latent where frames will be replaced.

height.tooltip: Target height in pixels. Set to 0 to auto-calculate from width while preserving aspect ratio.

crop.tooltip: How to handle aspect ratio mismatch: 'disabled' stretches to fit, 'center' crops to maintain aspect ratio.

multiplier.tooltip: Scale factor (e.g., 2.0 doubles size, 0.5 halves size).

longer_size.tooltip: The longer edge will be resized to this value. Aspect ratio is preserved.

input_color_space.tooltip:
Colorspace of the input tensor. The EXR is always written as scene-linear in the matching gamut.
  'sRGB'   — input is sRGB-encoded Rec.709; the inverse sRGB EOTF is applied.
  'HDR'    — input is HLG-encoded Rec.2020 (BT.2100); the inverse HLG OETF is applied to get scene-linear light.
  'linear' — input is already scene-linear (Rec.709 primaries); written through unchanged. Use this for renderer/compositor output.

MultiGPU_WorkUnits.name: MultiGPU_WorkUnits
MultiGPU_WorkUnits.description:
Prepares model to have sampling accelerated via splitting work units.

Should be placed after nodes that modify the model object itself, such as compile or attention-switch nodes.

Other than those exceptions, this node can be placed in any order.

SelectModelDevice.name: SelectModelDevice
SelectModelDevice.description:
Place the diffusion model on a specific device (default / cpu / gpu:N).

- "default" restores the device assigned by the loader (even after a
  prior Select Model Device call).
- "cpu" pins both the load and offload device to CPU.
- "gpu:N" pins the load device to the Nth available GPU; the offload
  device is restored to the loader's original choice.

When the requested device differs from the device the input model is
already on, a fresh model is spawned via the loader's reload factory
(cached_patcher_init) so the new patcher owns independent weights on
the new device. Loaders that don't support multigpu (no factory) will
cause the node to pass through unchanged with a warning.

If the workflow already has MultiGPU CFG Split applied and the chosen
GPU collides with one of the existing multigpu clones, that clone is
dropped so two patchers don't end up bound to the same device.

When the selected device does not exist on the current machine
(e.g. a workflow built on a 2-GPU box opened on a 1-GPU box),
the node passes the model through unchanged and logs a message
instead of failing.

NOTE: Placing Select Model Device *after* a node that has already
consumed the same model (e.g. a KSampler that ran on this model on
the original device) is not recommended -- any state the prior
consumer mutated on the original model will be observed when the
selected device matches the original (fast path). Place Select Model
Device before any consumer of the model.

SelectCLIPDevice.name: SelectCLIPDevice
SelectCLIPDevice.description:
Place the CLIP text encoder on a specific device (default / cpu / gpu:N).

- "default" restores the device assigned by the loader.
- "cpu" pins both the load and offload device to CPU.
- "gpu:N" pins the load device to the Nth available GPU.

When the selected device does not exist on the current machine
(e.g. a workflow built on a 2-GPU box opened on a 1-GPU box),
the node passes the CLIP through unchanged and logs a message
instead of failing.

SelectVAEDevice.name: SelectVAEDevice
SelectVAEDevice.description:
Place the VAE on a specific device (default / gpu:N).

- "default" restores the device assigned by the loader.
- "gpu:N" pins the load device to the Nth available GPU; the offload
  device is set to the standard VAE offload device.

CPU is intentionally not exposed in the UI for the VAE; if a workflow
supplies "cpu" anyway (e.g. opened from another machine), the request
is dropped with a log message and the VAE is passed through unchanged.

When the selected device does not exist on the current machine
(e.g. a workflow built on a 2-GPU box opened on a 1-GPU box),
the node passes the VAE through unchanged and logs a message
instead of failing.

CLIPTextEncodeLumina2.name: CLIPTextEncodeLumina2
CLIPTextEncodeLumina2.description: Encodes a system prompt and a user prompt using a CLIP model into an embedding that can be used to guide the diffusion model towards generating specific images.

video_frame_offset.tooltip: The amount of frames to seek in all the input videos. Used for generating longer videos by chunk. Connect to the video_frame_offset output of the previous node for extending a video.

mask_1.tooltip: Mask for the first speaker, required if using two audio inputs.

mask_2.tooltip: Mask for the second speaker, required if using two audio inputs.

motion_frame_count.tooltip: Number of previous frames to use as motion context.

pose_start.tooltip: Start step to use pose conditioning.

pose_end.tooltip: End step to use pose conditioning.

pose_video.tooltip: Video used for pose conditioning. Will be downscaled to half the resolution of the main video.

output_tooltips: Empty latent of the generation size.
WanSCAILToVideo.name: WanSCAILToVideo

source_video.tooltip: Source video to edit/restyle (task v2v or rv2v). Resized to width/height and trimmed to length. Acts as the edit base / canvas.

reference_video.tooltip: Moving content to composite into the source video (video insertion / ads2v),e.g. a clip to play on a screen. Kept at native aspect (long edge capped at ref_max_size), trimmed to length.

reference_images.tooltip: Reference image(s) injected as in-context tokens (task r2v or rv2v). Each slot is encoded independently at its own native aspect ratio (long edge capped at ref_max_size), so connect mixed-size references to separate slots.

reference_image.tooltip: A reference image injected as an in-context token (task r2v or rv2v).

ref_max_size.tooltip: Max size for the long edge of reference_video and reference_images. Resized with preserved aspect ratio and snapped to 16px (snapping may nudge a side by <16px).

BerniniConditioning.name: BerniniConditioning
BerniniConditioning.description: Conditioning node for Bernini in-context video/image conditioning. Attach source video and/or reference images to the positive/negative conditioning, which the Wan model will append as extra tokens with per-stream source_id rope.

batch_size.tooltip: The number of latent images in the batch.

VoxelToMeshBasic.name: VoxelToMeshBasic
VoxelToMeshBasic.description: Converts a voxel grid to a mesh.

VoxelToMesh.name: VoxelToMesh
VoxelToMesh.description: Converts a voxel grid to a mesh.

pre_cfg.tooltip: If true, rescale the combined noise BEFORE the sampler's CFG combine, without clamping (can amplify). Matches the norm-scaled CFG used by models like Lens. Default false keeps the original post-CFG x0-space attenuate-only behavior.

QuadrupleCLIPLoader.name: QuadrupleCLIPLoader
QuadrupleCLIPLoader.description:
[Recipes]

hidream: long clip-l, long clip-g, t5xxl, llama_8b_3.1_instruct

freq_cutoff.tooltip: Number of frequency indices around center to consider as low-frequency

FreSca.name: FreSca
FreSca.description: Applies frequency-dependent scaling to the guidance

eta.tooltip: Controls the scale of the parallel guidance vector. Default CFG behavior at a setting of 1.

norm_threshold.tooltip: Normalize guidance vector to this value, normalization disable at a setting of 0.

momentum.tooltip: Controls a running average of guidance during diffusion, disabled at a setting of 0.

batch_size.tooltip: The number of latent images in the batch.

generate_audio_codes.tooltip: Enable the LLM that generates audio codes. This can be slow but will increase the quality of the generated audio. Turn this off if you are giving the model an audio reference.

batch_size.tooltip: The number of latent images in the batch.

ReferenceTimbreAudio.name: ReferenceTimbreAudio
ReferenceTimbreAudio.description: This node sets the reference audio for ace step 1.5

StringFormat.name: StringFormat
StringFormat.description: Same as Python's string format method. Supports all of Python's format options and features.

dotall.tooltip: When enabled, the dot (.) character will match any character including newline characters. When disabled, dots won't match newlines.

count.tooltip: Maximum number of replacements to make. Set to 0 to replace all occurrences (default). Set to 1 to replace only the first match, 2 for the first two matches, etc.

RegexReplace.name: RegexReplace
RegexReplace.description: Find and replace text using regex patterns.

ReferenceLatent.name: ReferenceLatent
ReferenceLatent.description: This node sets the guiding latent for an edit model. If the model supports it you can chain multiple to set multiple reference images.

TCFG.name: TCFG
TCFG.description:
TCFG – Tangential Damping CFG (2503.18137)

Refine the uncond (negative) to align with the cond (positive) for improving quality.

model.tooltip: The model to apply context windows to during sampling.

context_length.tooltip: The length of the context window.

context_overlap.tooltip: The overlap of the context window.

context_schedule.tooltip: The stride of the context window.

context_stride.tooltip: The stride of the context window; only applicable to uniform schedules.

closed_loop.tooltip: Whether to close the context window loop; only applicable to looped schedules.

fuse_method.tooltip: The method to use to fuse the context windows.

dim.tooltip: The dimension to apply the context windows to.

freenoise.tooltip: Whether to apply FreeNoise noise shuffling, improves window blending.

cond_retain_index_list.tooltip: List of latent indices to retain in the conditioning tensors for each window, for example setting this to '0' will use the initial start image for each window.

split_conds_to_windows.tooltip: Whether to split multiple conditionings (created by ConditionCombine) to each window based on region index.

causal_window_fix.tooltip: Whether to add a causal fix frame to non-0-indexed context windows.

output_tooltips: The model with context windows applied during sampling.
ContextWindowsManual.name: ContextWindowsManual

model.tooltip: The model to apply context windows to during sampling.

context_length.tooltip: The length of the context window.

context_overlap.tooltip: The overlap of the context window.

context_schedule.tooltip: The stride of the context window.

context_stride.tooltip: The stride of the context window; only applicable to uniform schedules.

closed_loop.tooltip: Whether to close the context window loop; only applicable to looped schedules.

fuse_method.tooltip: The method to use to fuse the context windows.

freenoise.tooltip: Whether to apply FreeNoise noise shuffling, improves window blending.

output_tooltips: The model with context windows applied during sampling.
WanContextWindowsManual.name: WanContextWindowsManual
WanContextWindowsManual.description: Manually set context windows for WAN-like models (dim=2).

preserve_wrapper.tooltip: When enabled, will delegate to an existing model function wrapper if it exists. Generally should be left enabled.

start_sigma.tooltip: First sigma that these options will be in effect.

end_sigma.tooltip: Last sigma that these options will be in effect.

nerf_tile_size.tooltip: Allows overriding the default NeRF tile size. -1 means use the default (32). 0 means use non-tiling mode (may require a lot of VRAM).

force_sequential_txt_ids.tooltip: Force usage of sequential text token IDs instead of zeroes. Should be used for checkpoints from 2026-05-22 to 2026-06-01 that are trained in this way but do not contain the __sequential__ key in the state dict.

ChromaRadianceOptions.name: ChromaRadianceOptions
ChromaRadianceOptions.description: Allows setting advanced options for the Chroma Radiance model.

latent_format.tooltip: Flux1 and Flux2 latents auto-detected from channel dim, sd3 has to be selected manually.

degrade_sigma.tooltip: 0 = clean latent. Increase to denoise corrupted latent outputs.

PiDConditioning.name: PiDConditioning
PiDConditioning.description: Attaches a latent and a degrade_sigma scalar to a CONDITIONING for PiD decoding/upscaling

strength_start.tooltip: Control strength at the start of sampling (high sigma).

strength_end.tooltip: Control strength at the end of sampling (low sigma). Linearly interpolated from start.

restore_cfg.tooltip: Pulls denoised output toward the input latent. Higher = stronger fidelity to input. 0 to disable.

restore_cfg_s_tmin.tooltip: Sigma threshold below which restore_cfg is disabled.

model.tooltip: The model to add EasyCache to.

reuse_threshold.tooltip: The threshold for reusing cached steps.

start_percent.tooltip: The relative sampling step to begin use of EasyCache.

end_percent.tooltip: The relative sampling step to end use of EasyCache.

model.tooltip: The model to add LazyCache to.

reuse_threshold.tooltip: The threshold for reusing cached steps.

start_percent.tooltip: The relative sampling step to begin use of LazyCache.

end_percent.tooltip: The relative sampling step to end use of LazyCache.

LazyCache.name: LazyCache
LazyCache.description: A homebrew version of EasyCache - even 'easier' version of EasyCache to implement. Overall works worse than EasyCache, but better in some rare cases AND universal compatibility with everything in ComfyUI.

ScaleROPE.name: ScaleROPE
ScaleROPE.description: Scale and shift the ROPE of the model.

ComfyNotNode.name: ComfyNotNode
ComfyNotNode.description: Logical NOT operation. Returns true if the value is falsy. Uses Python's rules for truthiness.

ComfyAndNode.name: ComfyAndNode
ComfyAndNode.description: Logical AND operation. Returns true if all of the values are truthy. Uses Python's rules for truthiness.

ComfyOrNode.name: ComfyOrNode
ComfyOrNode.description: Logical OR operation. Returns true if any of the values are truthy. Uses Python's rules for truthiness.

aspect_ratio.tooltip: The aspect ratio for the output dimensions.

megapixels.tooltip: Target total megapixels. 1.0 MP ≈ 1024×1024 for square.

ResolutionSelector.name: ResolutionSelector
ResolutionSelector.description: Calculate width and height from aspect ratio and megapixel target. Useful for setting up Empty Latent Image dimensions.

wanBlockSwap.name: wanBlockSwap
wanBlockSwap.description: Intercept wanBlockSwap custom node that causes major instability and make it no-op.

output_tooltips: Clean encoded start images, used to replace the noisy start of the model output latents
Kandinsky5ImageToVideo.name: Kandinsky5ImageToVideo

start_frame_count.tooltip: Number of latent frames to normalize, counted from the start

reference_frame_count.tooltip: Number of latent frames after the start frames to use as reference

NormalizeVideoLatentStart.name: NormalizeVideoLatentStart
NormalizeVideoLatentStart.description: Normalizes the initial frames of a video latent to match the mean and standard deviation of subsequent reference frames. Helps reduce differences between the starting frames and the rest of the video.

start_x.tooltip: Normalized X coordinate (0-1) for start position.

start_y.tooltip: Normalized Y coordinate (0-1) for start position.

end_x.tooltip: Normalized X coordinate (0-1) for end position.

end_y.tooltip: Normalized Y coordinate (0-1) for end position.

track_spread.tooltip: Normalized distance between tracks. Tracks are spread perpendicular to the motion direction.

bezier.tooltip: Enable Bezier curve path using the mid point as control point.

mid_x.tooltip: Normalized X control point for Bezier curve. Only used when 'bezier' is enabled.

mid_y.tooltip: Normalized Y control point for Bezier curve. Only used when 'bezier' is enabled.

interpolation.tooltip: Controls the timing/speed of movement along the path.

track_mask.tooltip: Optional mask to indicate visible frames.

num_frame_per_block.tooltip: Frames per autoregressive block. 1 = framewise, 3 = chunkwise. Must match the checkpoint's training mode.

ImageCompare.name: ImageCompare
ImageCompare.description: Compares two images side by side with a slider.

fragment_shader.tooltip: GLSL fragment shader source code (GLSL ES 3.00 / WebGL 2.0 compatible)

size_mode.tooltip: Output size: 'from_input' uses first input image dimensions, 'custom' allows manual size

images.tooltip: Images are available as u_image0-4 (sampler2D) in the shader code

floats.tooltip: Floats are available as u_float0-19 in the shader code

ints.tooltip: Ints are available as u_int0-19 in the shader code

bools.tooltip: Booleans are available as u_bool0-9 (bool) in the shader code

curves.tooltip: Curves are available as u_curve0-3 (sampler2D, 1D LUT) in the shader code. Sample with texture(u_curve0, vec2(x, 0.5)).r

output_tooltips: Available via layout(location = 0) out vec4 fragColor0 in the shader code
Available via layout(location = 1) out vec4 fragColor1 in the shader code
Available via layout(location = 2) out vec4 fragColor2 in the shader code
Available via layout(location = 3) out vec4 fragColor3 in the shader code
GLSLShader.name: GLSLShader
GLSLShader.description: Apply GLSL ES fragment shaders to images. u_resolution (vec2) is always available.

model.tooltip: The diffusion model the LoRA will be applied to.

clip.tooltip: The CLIP model the LoRA will be applied to.

strength_model.tooltip: How strongly to modify the diffusion model. This value can be negative.

strength_clip.tooltip: How strongly to modify the CLIP model. This value can be negative.

LoraLoaderBypass.name: LoraLoaderBypass
LoraLoaderBypass.description: Apply LoRA in bypass mode. Unlike regular LoRA, this doesn't modify model weights - instead it injects the LoRA computation during forward pass. Useful for training scenarios.

LoraLoaderBypassModelOnly.name: LoraLoaderBypassModelOnly
LoraLoaderBypassModelOnly.description: Apply LoRA in bypass mode. Unlike regular LoRA, this doesn't modify model weights - instead it injects the LoRA computation during forward pass. Useful for training scenarios.

video.tooltip: Video frames as image batch. Assumed to be 24 FPS; subsampled to 1 FPS internally.

thinking.tooltip: Operate in thinking mode if the model supports it.

use_default_template.tooltip: Use the built in system prompt/template if the model has one.

video.tooltip: Video frames as image batch. Assumed to be 24 FPS; subsampled to 1 FPS internally.

thinking.tooltip: Operate in thinking mode if the model supports it.

use_default_template.tooltip: Use the built in system prompt/template if the model has one.

ColorToRGBInt.name: ColorToRGBInt
ColorToRGBInt.description: Convert a color to a RGB integer value.

model.tooltip: The model to apply NAG to.

nag_scale.tooltip: The guidance scale factor. Higher values push further from the negative prompt.

nag_alpha.tooltip: Blending factor for the normalized attention. 1.0 is full replacement, 0.0 is no effect.

output_tooltips: The patched model with NAG enabled.
NAGuidance.name: NAGuidance
NAGuidance.description: Applies Normalized Attention Guidance to models, enabling negative prompts on distilled/schnell models.

bboxes.tooltip: Optional bounding boxes for more accurate detections. Required for multi-person detection.

output_tooltips: Keypoints in OpenPose frame format (canvas_width, canvas_height, people)
SDPoseKeypointExtractor.name: SDPoseKeypointExtractor
SDPoseKeypointExtractor.description: Extract pose keypoints from images using the SDPose model: https://huggingface.co/Comfy-Org/SDPose/tree/main/checkpoints

scale.tooltip: Multiplier for the bounding box area around each detected face.

force_square.tooltip: Expand the shorter bbox axis so the crop region is always square.

output_tooltips: Face bounding boxes per frame, compatible with SDPoseKeypointExtractor bboxes input.
SDPoseFaceBBoxes.name: SDPoseFaceBBoxes

output_width.tooltip: Width each crop is resized to.

output_height.tooltip: Height each crop is resized to.

padding.tooltip: Extra padding in pixels added on each side of the bbox before cropping.

keep_aspect.tooltip: Whether to stretch the crop to fit the output size, or pad with black pixels to preserve aspect ratio.

output_tooltips: All crops stacked into a single image batch.
CropByBBoxes.name: CropByBBoxes
CropByBBoxes.description: Crop and resize regions from the input image batch based on provided bounding boxes.

image.tooltip: Optional base image to paint over

bg_removal_name.tooltip: The model used to remove backgrounds from images

image.tooltip: Input image to remove the background from

bg_removal_model.tooltip: Background removal model used to generate the mask

RemoveBackground.name: RemoveBackground
RemoveBackground.description: Generates a foreground mask to remove the background from an image using a background removal model.

class_name.tooltip: Filter detections by class. Set to 'all' to disable filtering.

max_detections.tooltip: Maximum number of detections to return per image. In order of descending confidence score.

model_name.tooltip: Select a frame interpolation model to load. Models must be placed in the 'frame_interpolation' folder.

refine_iterations.tooltip: SAM decoder refinement passes (0=use raw detector masks)

individual_masks.tooltip: Output per-object masks instead of union

positive_coords.tooltip: Positive point prompts as JSON [{"x": int, "y": int}, ...] (pixel coords)

negative_coords.tooltip: Negative point prompts as JSON [{"x": int, "y": int}, ...] (pixel coords)

images.tooltip: Video frames as batched images

max_objects.tooltip: Max tracked objects. Initial masks count toward this limit. 0 uses the internal cap of 64.

detect_interval.tooltip: Run detection every N frames (1=every frame). Higher values save compute.

initial_mask.tooltip: Mask(s) for the first frame to track (one per object)

conditioning.tooltip: Text conditioning for detecting new objects during tracking

object_indices.tooltip: Comma-separated object indices to include (e.g. '0,2,3'). Empty = all objects.

model_name.tooltip: Optical flow model to load.  Files must be placed in the 'optical_flow' folder.  Today only torchvision's raft_large.pth is supported.

dilate_width.tooltip: Dilation radius for the primary mask region (0 = no dilation)

quadmask.tooltip: Preprocessed quadmask from VOIDQuadmaskPreprocess [T, H, W]

length.tooltip: Number of pixel frames to process. For CogVideoX-Fun-V1.5 (patch_size_t=2), latent_t must be even — lengths that produce odd latent_t are rounded down (e.g. 49 → 45).

video.tooltip: Pass 1 output video frames [T, H, W, 3]

length.tooltip: Number of pixel frames. Rounded down to make latent_t even (patch_size_t=2 requirement), e.g. 49 → 45.
The number of frames in the generated video. Should stay 149 for WanDancer.

clip_vision_output.tooltip: The CLIP vision embeds for the first frame.

clip_vision_output_ref.tooltip: The CLIP vision embeds for the reference image.

start_image.tooltip: The initial image(s) to be encoded, can be any number of frames.

mask.tooltip: Image conditioning mask for the start image(s). White is kept, black is generated. Used for the local generations.

audio_inject_scale.tooltip: The scale for the audio features when injected into the video model.

output_tooltips: The calculated fps based on the audio length and the number of video frames. Used in the prompt.
WanDancerEncodeAudio.name: WanDancerEncodeAudio

segment_index.tooltip: Which segment this is (0 for first, 1 for second, etc.)

audio.tooltip: Audio to calculate total output frames from and extract segment audio.

output_tooltips: Audio segment for this video segment
WanDancerPadKeyframes.name: WanDancerPadKeyframes

num_segments.tooltip: How many padded segments to emit as lists.

audio.tooltip: Audio to slice for each emitted segment.

output_tooltips: Audio segment for each video segment
WanDancerPadKeyframesList.name: WanDancerPadKeyframesList

EmptyHiDreamO1LatentImage.name: EmptyHiDreamO1LatentImage
EmptyHiDreamO1LatentImage.description: Empty pixel-space latent for HiDream-O1-Image. The model was trained at ~4 megapixels; lower resolutions go off-distribution and quality regresses noticeably. Trained resolutions: 2048x2048, 2304x1728, 1728x2304, 2560x1440, 1440x2560, 2496x1664, 1664x2496, 3104x1312, 1312x3104, 2304x1792, 1792x2304.

images.tooltip: Reference images. 1 image = instruction edit; 2-10 images = multi reference.

HiDreamO1ReferenceImages.name: HiDreamO1ReferenceImages
HiDreamO1ReferenceImages.description: Attach 1-10 reference images to conditioning, one for edit instructionor multiple for subject-driven personalization.

start_percent.tooltip: Sampling progress (0=start, 1=end) at which the blend turns ON.

end_percent.tooltip: Sampling progress at which the blend turns OFF.

pattern.tooltip: Shift layout. single_shift: one pass at the natural patch grid + others offset. symmetric: all passes off-grid, shifts split around origin.

passes.tooltip: Number of passes per gated step. 2/4 = fixed. ramp_*: pass count increases as sampling approaches end (more smoothing where seams are most visible).

blend.tooltip: average: equal-weight mean. window: Hann-windowed weighting favoring each pass away from its patch boundaries. median: per-pixel median, rejects wraparound-outlier passes.

strength.tooltip: Interpolation between the natural-grid pred (0) and the averaged result (1).

HiDreamO1PatchSeamSmoothing.name: HiDreamO1PatchSeamSmoothing
HiDreamO1PatchSeamSmoothing.description: Average the model output across multiple shifted patch-grid positions during the late portion of sampling. Cancels seams.

fov_x_degrees.tooltip: Horizontal field of view of the source camera. Sets the focal length used to unproject the depth map into 3D. 0 = auto-recover from the predicted points.

batch_size.tooltip: Images per inference call. Lower if you OOM on a long video / image set.

apply_mask.tooltip: Set masked-out (sky / invalid) pixels to inf in points and depth so meshing culls them. Disable to keep the raw predicted geometry everywhere; the mask is still returned separately.

MoGeInference.name: MoGeInference
MoGeInference.description: Run MoGe on a single image to estimate depth and geometry.

image.tooltip: Equirectangular panorama (any aspect).

merge_resolution.tooltip: Long-side resolution of the merged equirect distance map.

batch_size.tooltip: Views per inference batch (12 splits total).

MoGePanoramaInference.name: MoGePanoramaInference
MoGePanoramaInference.description: Run MoGe on an equirectangular panorama by splitting it into 12 perspective views, running inference on each, and merging the results into a single depth map.

output.tooltip: DirectX vs OpenGL controls the normal-map green-channel convention. DirectX: green = -Y down (Unreal). OpenGL: green = +Y up (Blender, Substance, Unity, glTF).

MoGeRender.name: MoGeRender
MoGeRender.description: Render a depth map or normal map from geometry data

batch_index.tooltip: Which image of a batched MoGe geometry to mesh. Per-image vertex counts differ, so batches can't be stacked into a single MESH.

discontinuity_threshold.tooltip: Drop pixels whose 3x3 depth span exceeds this fraction. 0 = off.

texture.tooltip: Carry the source image through as the baseColor texture.

MoGePointMapToMesh.name: MoGePointMapToMesh
MoGePointMapToMesh.display_name: Convert MoGe Point Map to Mesh
MoGePointMapToMesh.description: Convert a MoGe point map into a 3D mesh.

detector_variant.tooltip: Face detector range. 'short' is tuned for close-up faces (within ~2 m of the camera); 'full' covers farther / smaller faces (up to ~5 m) but is slower. 'both' runs both detectors and keeps whichever found more faces per frame (~2× detection cost).

num_faces.tooltip: Maximum faces to return per frame. 0 = no cap (return all detected).

min_confidence.tooltip: BlazeFace score threshold. Lower to catch small/occluded faces.

missing_frame_fallback.tooltip: Per-frame behaviour when detection fails in a batch. 'empty' leaves the frame faceless. 'previous' copies the most recent successful detection. 'interpolate' lerps landmarks/bbox/blendshapes between bracketing successful frames. Multi-face: pairs faces across frames by greedy bbox-centre NN.

MediaPipeFaceLandmarker.name: MediaPipeFaceLandmarker
MediaPipeFaceLandmarker.description: Detects facial landmarks using MediaPipe model.

connections.tooltip: 'all' = oval+eyes+brows+lips+irises+nose. 'fill' = solid face_oval polygon (silhouette mask). 'custom' = toggle each feature individually (including 'tesselation', the full 2547-edge wireframe).

thickness.tooltip: Edge line thickness in pixels. 0 disables edge drawing.

point_size.tooltip: Landmark dot radius in pixels. 0 disables point drawing.

image.tooltip: If not connected, a black canvas will be used.

MediaPipeFaceMeshVisualize.name: MediaPipeFaceMeshVisualize
MediaPipeFaceMeshVisualize.description: Draws face landmarks mesh on the input image.

regions.tooltip: 'all' = union of face_oval+lips+eyes+irises (which collapses to face_oval since it encloses the rest). 'custom' = toggle each region individually for combos like lips+eyes.

face_oval.tooltip: Include the 'face_oval' region in the mask.

lips.tooltip: Include the 'lips' region in the mask.

left_eye.tooltip: Include the 'left_eye' region in the mask.

right_eye.tooltip: Include the 'right_eye' region in the mask.

irises.tooltip: Include the 'irises' region in the mask.

MediaPipeFaceMask.name: MediaPipeFaceMask
MediaPipeFaceMask.description: Draws a mask from face landmarks.

format.tooltip: ply: standard 3D Gaussian Splat with full spherical harmonics. ksplat: mkkellogg SplatBuffer (level 0, uncompressed), base color only spz: Niantic gzip-compressed (~10x smaller), base color only

SplatToFile3D.name: SplatToFile3D
SplatToFile3D.description: Serialize a gaussian splat to a File3D object for Save / Preview 3D nodes. Supports one item per batch only.

File3DToSplat.name: File3DToSplat
File3DToSplat.description: Parse a splat File3D into a gaussian splat. Inverse of Create 3D File (from Splat). Supported format:  PLY, SPLAT, KSPLAT, SPZ. PLY carries full spherical harmonics, the other formats are base color only. Format is auto-detected from the file contents.

frames.tooltip: -1, 0, 1 = single still image; >1 = turntable, the camera orbits over a full 360 turn (works with any camera_info). Negative value orbits the other way.

splat_scale.tooltip: Multiplier on each splat's projected footprint (lower = crisper points, higher = softer/fuller surface).

sharpen.tooltip: Sharpen overlapping splats: 1.0 = physically-correct blend; higher biases each pixel toward its dominant (nearest) splat for crisper texture, without shrinking splats or opening gaps. Non-physical above 1.

headlight_shading.tooltip: Diffuse shading from a light at the camera (headlight), using the splat surfel normals: darkens surfaces that turn away from view to reveal form/curvature. 0 = flat albedo, 1 = strongest shading.

opacity_threshold.tooltip: Cull gaussians with opacity below this (removes faint floaters).

render_style.tooltip: What the image output shows: color, clay (neutral-albedo shaded), depth (near=bright), normal (OpenGL normal map).

bg_image.tooltip: Optional background plate composited behind the splat (overrides the solid background colour). Resized to the render size; a batch is used per frame, a single image for all. color/clay only.

camera_info.tooltip: Camera to render from - a Load3D / Preview3D camera or a Create Camera Info node. If empty, the splat is auto-framed from a default 3/4 view.

RenderSplat.name: RenderSplat
RenderSplat.description: Render a gaussian splat as an image with an anisotropic EWA rasterizer (oriented elliptical splats, antialiased, depth-sorted front-to-back). The camera comes from a camera_info input (Load / Preview 3D, or a Create Camera Info node); leave it empty to auto-frame the splat. Set frames greater than 1 for a turntable batch of images to feed a Video node.

mode.tooltip: How to define the camera: orbit angles, an explicit position, or a position + quaternion.

position_x.tooltip: Camera position in world space (right-handed, Y-up).
Camera position in world space (right-handed, Y-up).

quat_w.tooltip: Camera world-rotation quaternion (three.js: looks down local -Z). Normalized for you.

target_x.tooltip: Look-at point (orbit pivot / aim). In orbit mode, move it to pan/translate the whole camera. Ignored in quaternion mode. Defaults to the origin.

roll.tooltip: Camera roll about the view axis, degrees.

fov.tooltip: Vertical field of view in degrees.

zoom.tooltip: Digital zoom (focal-length multiplier). >1 zooms in without moving the camera.

camera_type.tooltip: Projection used by Render Splat: perspective (foreshortening) or orthographic (parallel).

CreateCameraInfo.name: CreateCameraInfo
CreateCameraInfo.description: Build a camera_infoMode 'orbit' aims with yaw/pitch/distance around the target; 'look_at' places the camera at world position. Coordinates are the viewer's world space (right-handed,Y-up).

TransformSplat.name: TransformSplat
TransformSplat.description: Translate, rotate, and scale a gaussian splat. Non-uniform scale also reshapes every individual splat, slower process.

GetSplatCount.name: GetSplatCount
GetSplatCount.description: Returns the number of splats summed across the batch.

MergeSplat.name: MergeSplat
MergeSplat.description: Concatenate any number of gaussian splats into one. Unioning several decodes of the same latent at different seeds densifies the surface, this can improve surface quality when meshing.

resolution.tooltip: Density-grid resolution along the longest axis. Higher = finer surface, more VRAM/time (grows with resolution^3).

kernel.tooltip: Max splat half-width in voxels. Each gaussian is rasterized over a window sized to its own 3-sigma, capped here - small surfels stay cheap, large ones aren't truncated. Raise if sparse splats leave gaps.

smooth.tooltip: Taubin mesh-smoothing iterations. Smooths the surface without shrinking it (volume-preserving), unlike blurring the density. 0 = raw surface.

level.tooltip: Iso-surface level. Auto-picked by Otsu; this biases it (1.0 = auto, lower = fatter/more-connected surface, higher = thinner/tighter).

min_component.tooltip: Drop connected components smaller than this many vertices (0 = keep all). Removes detached floater blobs and the inner shell of the double wall.

min_opacity.tooltip: Ignore gaussians fainter than this before meshing.

color_sharpen.tooltip: Crisp up the vertex texture: 1.0 = physically-correct blend; higher biases each voxel's colour toward its dominant gaussian instead of averaging neighbours (de-smears the texture). Colour only - geometry is unchanged.

SplatToMesh.name: SplatToMesh
SplatToMesh.description: Extract a coloured mesh from a gaussian splat.

erode_radius.tooltip: Erode the alpha matte by this pixel radius before cropping (avoids border bleed).

size.tooltip: Square image size. The model is trained at 1024; other sizes run but are off-distribution.

TripoSplatPreprocessImage.name: TripoSplatPreprocessImage
TripoSplatPreprocessImage.description: Crop center each image to a square canvas on a black background and add padding.

TripoSplatConditioning.name: TripoSplatConditioning
TripoSplatConditioning.description: Encode the image with DINOv3 and the Flux2 VAE into TripoSplat positive/negative conditioning, and create the fixed size noise target (latent + camera) for the KSampler

num_gaussians.tooltip: Number of gaussians to produce (rounded to a multiple of 32). 262144 matches the octree's point density; higher oversamples the same points (denser, but no new detail) and costs proportionally more VRAM/time.

seed.tooltip: Seeds the octree point sampler (global RNG) for deterministic decodes.

VAEDecodeTripoSplat.name: VAEDecodeTripoSplat
VAEDecodeTripoSplat.description: Decode the sampled TripoSplat latent into a 3D gaussian splat. Modify the number of gaussians to vary the density.

octree_level.tooltip: Octree depth for the preview decode (lower = cheaper/coarser).

num_gaussians.tooltip: Number of gaussians to produce for the preview (rounded to a multiple of 32).

point_size.tooltip: Maximum splat radius in pixels. Each gaussian is sized from its scale and capped here; lower = finer/pointier, higher = chunkier.

TripoSplatSamplingPreview.name: TripoSplatSamplingPreview
TripoSplatSamplingPreview.description: Patch the TripoSplat model for the standard Ksampler node to show a live decoded gaussian splat preview at each step.

alpha_mode.tooltip: Controls how SwitchX decides what to keep vs. regenerate. 'auto' isolates the main subject automatically. 'fill' regenerates the entire frame while preserving geometry. alpha_keyframe.tooltip: First-frame keyframe mask. Beeble propagates this across the video.

alpha_mask.tooltip: Per-frame grayscale mask covering the entire video. Must have the same frame count as the source. Connect a MASK output from SAM3_TrackToMask or similar.

seed.tooltip: Seed controls whether the node should re-run; results are non-deterministic regardless of seed.

output_tooltips: The alpha matte Beeble used. Empty for 'fill' mode, which has no separate matte.

ElevenLabsTextToDialogue.name: ElevenLabsTextToDialogue
ElevenLabsTextToDialogue.description: Generate multi-speaker dialogue from text. Each dialogue entry has its own text and voice.

prompt.tooltip: Text inputs to the model, used to generate a response. You can include detailed instructions, questions, or context for the model.

model.tooltip: The Gemini model to use for generating responses.

seed.tooltip: When seed is fixed to a specific value, the model makes a best effort to provide the same response for repeated requests. Deterministic output isn't guaranteed. Also, changing the model or parameter settings, such as the temperature, can cause variations in the response even when you use the same seed value. By default, a random seed value is used.

images.tooltip: Optional image(s) to use as context for the model. To include multiple images, you can use the Batch Images node.

audio.tooltip: Optional audio to use as context for the model.

video.tooltip: Optional video to use as context for the model.

files.tooltip: Optional file(s) to use as context for the model. Accepts inputs from the Gemini Generate Content Input Files node.

system_prompt.tooltip: Foundational instructions that dictate an AI's behavior.

GeminiNode.name: GeminiNode
GeminiNode.description: Generate text responses with Google's Gemini AI model. You can provide multiple types of inputs (text, images, audio, video) as context for generating more relevant and meaningful responses.

prompt.tooltip: Text prompt for generation

model.tooltip: The Gemini model to use for generating responses.

seed.tooltip: When seed is fixed to a specific value, the model makes a best effort to provide the same response for repeated requests. Deterministic output isn't guaranteed. Also, changing the model or parameter settings, such as the temperature, can cause variations in the response even when you use the same seed value. By default, a random seed value is used.

images.tooltip: Optional image(s) to use as context for the model. To include multiple images, you can use the Batch Images node.

files.tooltip: Optional file(s) to use as context for the model. Accepts inputs from the Gemini Generate Content Input Files node.

aspect_ratio.tooltip: Defaults to matching the output image size to that of your input image, or otherwise generates 1:1 squares.

response_modalities.tooltip: Choose 'IMAGE' for image-only output, or 'IMAGE+TEXT' to return both the generated image and a text response.

system_prompt.tooltip: Foundational instructions that dictate an AI's behavior.
system_prompt.default:
You are an expert image-generation engine. You must ALWAYS produce an image.
Interpret all user input—regardless of format, intent, or abstraction—as literal visual directives for image composition.
If a prompt is conversational or lacks specific visual details, you must creatively invent a concrete visual scenario that depicts the concept.
Prioritize generating the visual representation above any text, formatting, or conversational requests.

GeminiImageNode.name: GeminiImageNode
GeminiImageNode.description: Edit images synchronously via Google API.

prompt.tooltip: Text prompt describing the image to generate or the edits to apply. Include any constraints, styles, or details the model should follow.

seed.tooltip: When the seed is fixed to a specific value, the model makes a best effort to provide the same response for repeated requests. Deterministic output isn't guaranteed. Also, changing the model or parameter settings, such as the temperature, can cause variations in the response even when you use the same seed value. By default, a random seed value is used.

aspect_ratio.tooltip: If set to 'auto', matches your input image's aspect ratio; if no image is provided, a 16:9 square is usually generated.

resolution.tooltip: Target output resolution. For 2K/4K the native Gemini upscaler is used.

response_modalities.tooltip: Choose 'IMAGE' for image-only output, or 'IMAGE+TEXT' to return both the generated image and a text response.

images.tooltip: Optional reference image(s). To include multiple images, use the Batch Images node (up to 14).

files.tooltip: Optional file(s) to use as context for the model. Accepts inputs from the Gemini Generate Content Input Files node.

system_prompt.tooltip: Foundational instructions that dictate an AI's behavior.
system_prompt.default:
You are an expert image-generation engine. You must ALWAYS produce an image.
Interpret all user input—regardless of format, intent, or abstraction—as literal visual directives for image composition.
If a prompt is conversational or lacks specific visual details, you must creatively invent a concrete visual scenario that depicts the concept.
Prioritize generating the visual representation above any text, formatting, or conversational requests.

horizontal_movement.tooltip: Controls camera's movement along horizontal axis (x-axis). Negative indicates left, positive indicates right

vertical_movement.tooltip: Controls camera's movement along vertical axis (y-axis). Negative indicates downward, positive indicates upward.

pan.tooltip: Controls camera's rotation in vertical plane (x-axis). Negative indicates downward rotation, positive indicates upward rotation.

tilt.tooltip: Controls camera's rotation in horizontal plane (y-axis). Negative indicates left rotation, positive indicates right rotation.

roll.tooltip: Controls camera's rolling amount (z-axis). Negative indicates counterclockwise, positive indicates clockwise.

zoom.tooltip: Controls change in camera's focal length. Negative indicates narrower field of view, positive indicates wider field of view.

KlingLipSyncAudioToVideoNode.name: KlingLipSyncAudioToVideoNode
KlingLipSyncAudioToVideoNode.display_name: Kling Lip Sync Video with Audio
KlingLipSyncAudioToVideoNode.description: Kling Lip Sync Audio to Video Node. Syncs mouth movements in a video file to the audio content of an audio file. When using, ensure that the audio contains clearly distinguishable vocals and that the video contains a distinct face. The audio file should not be larger than 5MB. The video file should not be larger than 100MB, should have height/width between 720px and 1920px, and should be between 2s and 10s in length.

text.tooltip: Text Content for Lip-Sync Video Generation. Required when mode is text2video. Maximum length is 120 characters.

voice_speed.tooltip: Speech Rate. Valid range: 0.8~2.0, accurate to one decimal place.

KlingLipSyncTextToVideoNode.description: Kling Lip Sync Text to Video Node. Syncs mouth movements in a video file to a text prompt. The video file should not be larger than 100MB, should have height/width between 720px and 1920px, and should be between 2s and 10s in length.

reference_video.tooltip:
Motion reference video used to drive movement/expression.
Duration limits depend on character_orientation:
 - image: 3–10s (max 10s)
 - video: 3–30s (max 30s)

character_orientation.tooltip:
Controls where the character's facing/orientation comes from.
video: movements, expressions, camera moves, and orientation follow the motion reference video (other details via prompt).
image: movements and expressions still follow the motion reference video, but the character orientation matches the reference image (camera/other details via prompt).

MagnificImageUpscalerCreativeNode.name: MagnificImageUpscalerCreativeNode
MagnificImageUpscalerCreativeNode.description: Prompt‑guided enhancement, stylization, and 2x/4x/8x/16x upscaling. Maximum output: 25.3 megapixels.

flavor.tooltip: Processing style: sublime for general use, photo for photographs, photo_denoiser for noisy photos.

sharpen.tooltip: Image sharpness intensity. Higher values increase edge definition and clarity.

smart_grain.tooltip: Intelligent grain/texture enhancement to prevent the image from looking too smooth or artificial.

auto_downscale.tooltip: Automatically downscale input image if output would exceed maximum resolution.

MagnificImageUpscalerPreciseV2Node.name: MagnificImageUpscalerPreciseV2Node
MagnificImageUpscalerPreciseV2Node.description: High-fidelity upscaling with fine control over sharpness, grain, and detail. Maximum output: 10060×10060 pixels.

image.tooltip: The image to apply style transfer to.

structure_strength.tooltip: Maintains the structure of the original image.

portrait_mode.tooltip: Enable portrait mode for facial enhancements.

portrait_style.tooltip: Visual style applied to portrait images.

fixed_generation.tooltip: When disabled, expect each generation to introduce a degree of randomness, leading to more diverse outcomes.

MagnificImageStyleTransferNode.name: MagnificImageStyleTransferNode
MagnificImageStyleTransferNode.description: Transfer the style from a reference image to your input image.

preserve_details.tooltip: Maintains texture and fine details from original.

advanced_settings.tooltip: Fine-tuning options for advanced lighting control.

whites.tooltip: Adjusts the brightest tones in the image.

blacks.tooltip: Adjusts the darkest tones in the image.

transfer_light_b.tooltip: Also modifies light transfer intensity. Can be combined with the previous control for varied effects.

fixed_generation.tooltip: Ensures consistent output with the same settings.

reference_image.tooltip: Optional reference image to transfer lighting from.

pose_mode.tooltip: Specify the pose mode for the generated model.

MeshyAnimateModelNode.name: MeshyAnimateModelNode

RunwayFirstLastFrameNode.name: RunwayFirstLastFrameNode
RunwayFirstLastFrameNode.description: Upload first and last keyframes, draft a prompt, and generate a video. More complex transitions, such as cases where the Last frame is completely different from the First frame, may benefit from the longer 10s duration. This would give the generation more time to smoothly transition between the two inputs. Before diving in, review these best practices to ensure that your input selections will set your generation up for success: https://help.runwayml.com/hc/en-us/articles/34170748696595-Creating-with-Keyframes-on-Gen-3.

OpenAI video and audio generation.

DEPRECATION NOTICE: OpenAI will stop serving the Sora v2 API in September 2026. This node will be removed from ComfyUI at that time.

prompt.tooltip: What you wish to see in the output image. A strong, descriptive prompt that clearly defineselements, colors, and subjects will lead to better results. To control the weight of a given word use the format `(word:weight)`,where `word` is the word you'd like to control the weight of and `weight`is a value between 0 and 1. For example: `The sky was a crisp (blue:0.3) and (green:0.8)`would convey a sky that was blue and green, but more green than blue.

color.tooltip: Color as RGB/RGBA values in range 0-255 or 0.0-1.0, separated by commas. Ex: 255, 0, 0, 128

DrawMaskOnImage.name: DrawMaskOnImage
DrawMaskOnImage.description: Applies the provided masks to the input images with Alpha Blending support.

DownloadAndLoadCLIPSeg.name: DownloadAndLoadCLIPSeg
DownloadAndLoadCLIPSeg.description:
Downloads and loads CLIPSeg model with huggingface_hub,  
to ComfyUI/models/clip_seg

BatchCLIPSeg.name: BatchCLIPSeg
BatchCLIPSeg.description: Segments an image or batch of images using CLIPSeg.

block_size.tooltip: Size of blocks in pixels (smaller = smaller blocks)

BlockifyMask.name: BlockifyMask
BlockifyMask.description: Creates a block mask by dividing the bounding box of each mask into blocks of the specified size and filling in blocks that contain any part of the original mask.

ColorToMask.name: ColorToMask
ColorToMask.description:
Converts chosen RGB value to a mask.  
With batch inputs, the **per_batch**  
controls the number of images processed at once.

CreateTextMask.name: CreateTextMask
CreateTextMask.description:
Creates a text image and mask.  
Looks for fonts from this folder:  
ComfyUI/custom_nodes/ComfyUI-KJNodes/fonts
  
If start_rotation and/or end_rotation are different values,  
creates animation between them.

CreateFadeMaskAdvanced.name: CreateFadeMaskAdvanced
CreateFadeMaskAdvanced.description:
Create a batch of masks interpolated between given frames and values. 
Uses same syntax as Fizz' BatchValueSchedule.
First value is the frame index (not that this starts from 0, not 1) 
and the second value inside the brackets is the float value of the mask in range 0.0 - 1.0  

For example the default values:  
0:(0.0)  
7:(1.0)  
15:(0.0)  
  
Would create a mask batch fo 16 frames, starting from black, 
interpolating with the chosen curve to fully white at the 8th frame, 
and interpolating from that to fully black at the 16th frame.

CreateShapeMask.name: CreateShapeMask
CreateShapeMask.description:
Creates a mask or batch of masks with the specified shape.  
Locations are center locations.  
Grow value is the amount to grow the shape on each frame, creating animated masks.

GetMaskSizeAndCount.name: GetMaskSizeAndCount
GetMaskSizeAndCount.description:
Returns the width, height and batch size of the mask,  
and passes it through unchanged.

GrowMaskWithBlur.name: GrowMaskWithBlur
GrowMaskWithBlur.description:
# GrowMaskWithBlur
- mask: Input mask or mask batch
- expand: Expand or contract mask or mask batch by a given amount
- incremental_expandrate: increase expand rate by a given amount per frame
- tapered_corners: use tapered corners
- flip_input: flip input mask
- blur_radius: value higher than 0 will blur the mask
- lerp_alpha: alpha value for interpolation between frames
- decay_factor: decay value for interpolation between frames
- fill_holes: fill holes in the mask (slow)

MaskBatchMulti.name: MaskBatchMulti
MaskBatchMulti.description:
Creates an image batch from multiple masks.  
You can set how many inputs the node has,  
with the **inputcount** and clicking update.

OffsetMask.name: OffsetMask
OffsetMask.description:
Offsets the mask by the specified amount.  
 - mask: Input mask or mask batch
 - x: Horizontal offset
 - y: Vertical offset
 - angle: Angle in degrees
 - roll: roll edge wrapping
 - duplication_factor: Number of times to duplicate the mask to form a batch
 - border padding_mode: Padding mode for the mask

RemapMaskRange.name: RemapMaskRange
RemapMaskRange.description: Sets new min and max values for the mask.

ResizeMask.name: ResizeMask
ResizeMask.description: Resizes the mask or batch of masks to the specified width and height.

RoundMask.name: RoundMask
RoundMask.description: Rounds the mask or batch of masks to a binary mask.

SeparateMasks.name: SeparateMasks
SeparateMasks.description: Separates a mask into multiple masks based on the size of the connected components.

ConsolidateMasksKJ.name: ConsolidateMasksKJ
ConsolidateMasksKJ.description: Consolidates a batch of separate masks by finding the largest group of masks that fit inside a tile of the given width and height (including the padding), and repeating until no more masks can be combined.

AddLabel.name: AddLabel
AddLabel.description:
Creates a new with the given text, and concatenates it to  
either above or below the input image.  
Note that this changes the input image's height!  
Fonts are loaded from this folder:  
ComfyUI/custom_nodes/ComfyUI-KJNodes/fonts

ColorMatch.name: ColorMatch
ColorMatch.description:
color-matcher enables color transfer across images which comes in handy for automatic  
color-grading of photographs, paintings and film sequences as well as light-field  
and stopmotion corrections.  

The methods behind the mappings are based on the approach from Reinhard et al.,  
the Monge-Kantorovich Linearization (MKL) as proposed by Pitie et al. and our analytical solution  
to a Multi-Variate Gaussian Distribution (MVGD) transfer in conjunction with classical histogram   
matching. As shown below our HM-MVGD-HM compound outperforms existing methods.   
https://github.com/hahnec/color-matcher/

ColorMatchV2.name: ColorMatchV2
ColorMatchV2.description:
color-matcher enables color transfer across images which comes in handy for automatic  
color-grading of photographs, paintings and film sequences as well as light-field  
and stopmotion corrections.  

The methods behind the mappings are based on the approach from Reinhard et al.,  
the Monge-Kantorovich Linearization (MKL) as proposed by Pitie et al. and our analytical solution  
to a Multi-Variate Gaussian Distribution (MVGD) transfer in conjunction with classical histogram   
matching. As shown below our HM-MVGD-HM compound outperforms existing methods.   
https://github.com/hahnec/color-matcher/   

'reinhard_lab_gpu' method uses Kornia for GPU-accelerated color transfer in Lab color space.

ImageTensorList.name: ImageTensorList
ImageTensorList.description: Creates an image list from the input images.

GetImagesFromBatchIndexed.name: GetImagesFromBatchIndexed
GetImagesFromBatchIndexed.description: Selects and returns the images at the specified indices as an image batch.

GetImageRangeFromBatch.name: GetImageRangeFromBatch
GetImageRangeFromBatch.display_name: Get Image or Mask Range From Batch
GetImageRangeFromBatch.description: Returns a range of images from a batch.

input.tooltip: Image or mask batch to sample from.

start_index.tooltip: Inclusive start of the sampling range. Negative values count from the end.

end_index.tooltip: Inclusive end of the sampling range. -1 means the last frame.

num_frames.tooltip: How many frames to pick from the range.

randomness.tooltip: 0 = evenly spaced across the range, 1 = uniformly random without replacement, in-between = linear blend (jittered even spacing).

min_distance.tooltip: Minimum gap (in frames) between consecutive picks. 0 = no minimum. Picks are pushed forward to satisfy this; later picks may clamp to the range end.

max_distance.tooltip: Maximum gap (in frames) between consecutive picks. 0 = no maximum. Picks are pulled in to satisfy this, which may compress the sequence toward the start.

seed.tooltip: Random seed for reproducible sampling. Ignored when randomness is 0.

RandomImageFromBatch.name: RandomImageFromBatch
RandomImageFromBatch.description: Picks a sequence of frames from an image or mask batch within a selected index range. At randomness=0 the picks are evenly spaced across the range; at randomness=1 they are uniformly random without replacement; values in between blend linearly. Output is always sorted by batch index. Negative indices count from the end (-1 = last).

GetLatentRangeFromBatch.name: GetLatentRangeFromBatch
GetLatentRangeFromBatch.description: Returns a range of latents from a batch.

GetLatentSizeAndCount.name: GetLatentSizeAndCount
GetLatentSizeAndCount.description:
Returns latent tensor dimensions,  
and passes the latent through unchanged.

GetImageSizeAndCount.name: GetImageSizeAndCount
GetImageSizeAndCount.description:
Returns width, height and batch size of the image,  
and passes it through unchanged.

max_size.tooltip: Maximum width or height for the preview. Images larger than this are downscaled before encoding.

FastPreview.name: FastPreview
FastPreview.description: Fast image preview using binary websocket, bypassing base64/JSON overhead.

input.tooltip: Image or mask batch to preview.

max_thumb_size.tooltip: Detail-view (mp4) thumbnail max side. Strip thumbs for the grid are auto-capped at 256.

crf.tooltip: H.264 CRF. Lower = higher quality / larger file.

max_grid_frames.tooltip: If batch exceeds this, frames are stride-sampled evenly.

FastPreviewBatch.name: FastPreviewBatch
FastPreviewBatch.description: Encodes an image batch as an all-I-frame H.264 MP4 thumbnail strip and shows it as an interactive grid. Click a tile to enlarge with prev/next browsing. Avoids materializing N PNGs.

ImageBatchFilter.name: ImageBatchFilter
ImageBatchFilter.description: Removes empty images from a batch

ImageAndMaskPreview.name: ImageAndMaskPreview
ImageAndMaskPreview.description:
Preview an image or a mask, when both inputs are used  
composites the mask on top of the image.
with pass_through on the preview is disabled and the  
composite is returned from the composite slot instead,  
this allows for the preview to be passed for video combine  
nodes for example. Supports RGBA for mask_color to adjust transparency per color.

ImageAddMulti.name: ImageAddMulti
ImageAddMulti.description:
Add blends multiple images together.    
You can set how many inputs the node has,  
with the **inputcount** and clicking update.

ImageBatchJoinWithTransition.name: ImageBatchJoinWithTransition
ImageBatchJoinWithTransition.description:
Transitions between two batches of images, starting at a specified index in the first batch.
During the transition, frames from both batches are blended frame-by-frame, so the video keeps playing.

ImageBatchMulti.name: ImageBatchMulti
ImageBatchMulti.description:
Creates an image batch from multiple images.  
You can set how many inputs the node has,  
with the **inputcount** and clicking update.

ImageBatchRepeatInterleaving.name: ImageBatchRepeatInterleaving
ImageBatchRepeatInterleaving.description:
Repeats each image in a batch by the specified number of times.  
Example batch of 5 images: 0, 1 ,2, 3, 4  
with repeats 2 becomes batch of 10 images: 0, 0, 1, 1, 2, 2, 3, 3, 4, 4

ImageBatchTestPattern.name: ImageBatchTestPattern
ImageBatchTestPattern.description: Generate a batch of images with sequential numbers rendered in a chosen font.

ImageConcanate.name: ImageConcanate
ImageConcanate.description:
Concatenates image2 to image1 in the specified direction.
Both inputs accept IMAGE or MASK; the output type follows image1.
If image2 is a different type than image1 it's converted (RGB mean for image→mask,
channel-replicate for mask→image).
When match_image_size is False and dimensions don't match along the shared axis,
the smaller image is centered and zero-padded instead of erroring.

ImageConcatFromBatch.name: ImageConcatFromBatch
ImageConcatFromBatch.description: Concatenates images from a batch into a grid with a specified number of columns.

ImageConcatMulti.name: ImageConcatMulti
ImageConcatMulti.description:
Creates an image from multiple images or masks.
Set the input count and click 'Update inputs' to add more slots.
The output type follows image_1; other inputs are converted to match.

ImageCropByMask.name: ImageCropByMask
ImageCropByMask.description: Crops the input images based on the provided mask.

ImageCropByMaskAndResize.name: ImageCropByMaskAndResize
ImageCropByMaskAndResize.display_name: Image Crop By Mask And Resize

ImageCropByMaskBatch.description: Crops the input images based on the provided masks.

ImageBatchExtendWithOverlap.description:

First input source and overlap amount to get the starting frames for the extension.

ImageResizeKJv2.name: ImageResizeKJv2

MergeImageChannels.description: Merges channel data into an image.

video_latent.tooltip: The latent representation of the video frames.

filename_prefix.tooltip: The prefix for the file to save. This may include formatting information such as %date:yyyy-MM-dd% or %Empty Latent Image.width% to include values from nodes.

tile_size.tooltip: Size of the tiles to decode. Smaller tiles use less memory but take more time.

temporal_overlap.tooltip: Only used for video VAEs: Amount of frames to overlap. Higher overlap can improve quality at the edges of temporal tiles but uses more memory and takes more time.

audio_latent.tooltip: The latent representation of the audio frames.

DecodeAndSaveVideo.name: DecodeAndSaveVideo
DecodeAndSaveVideo.description: Decodes video frames and audio from latent representations, combines them, and saves as a video file, without keeping intermediate images in memory.

image.tooltip: The image or mask to transform.

target_width.tooltip: Target output width. 0 = keep original dimensions.

target_height.tooltip: Target output height. 0 = keep original dimensions.

pad_x.tooltip: Horizontal position of content within padding (0=left, 0.5=center, 1=right). Shift+drag content in preview to adjust.

pad_y.tooltip: Vertical position of content within padding (0=top, 0.5=center, 1=bottom). Shift+drag content in preview to adjust.

edge_mode.tooltip: clamp: extend edge pixels. repeat: tile the image. mirror: tile with mirroring.

pad_x.tooltip: Horizontal position of content within padding (0=left, 0.5=center, 1=right). Shift+drag content in preview to adjust.

pad_y.tooltip: Vertical position of content within padding (0=top, 0.5=center, 1=bottom). Shift+drag content in preview to adjust.

width_mult.tooltip: Multiply the crop width by this factor.

height_mult.tooltip: Multiply the crop height by this factor.

edge_mode.tooltip: clamp: extend edge pixels. repeat: tile the image. mirror: tile with mirroring.
clamp: extend edge pixels. repeat: tile the image. mirror: tile with mirroring.

mask.tooltip: Optional mask to transform alongside the image.

ImageTransformKJ.name: ImageTransformKJ
ImageTransformKJ.description:
Interactive image transform node: crop, resize, pad, and rotate.  
Connect an image input — the preview appears automatically.  

Cropping:  
Click + drag to draw a crop region.  
Drag inside to move, drag edges/corners to resize.  
Right-click to delete a region.  
Ctrl to snap to grid.  
Shift + resize to constrain aspect ratio.  
Alt + resize to resize symmetrically.  

Padding:  
Shift + drag to adjust padding position.

Rotate button enables rotation cross (drag to rotate, right-click to reset).  
Set target_width/height to resize output (0 = keep original).  
Use keep_proportion to control how the image fits the target.  
Use extra_padding to add padding with color or edge fill (clamp/repeat/mirror).

image.tooltip: LogC3-compressed HDR image/video in [0,1], or linear HDR if 'input_space' is 'linear'.

exposure.tooltip: Exposure in EV stops. 0 = no change, +1 = 2x brighter.

fps.tooltip: Playback frame rate for video (batch) inputs.

input_space.tooltip: Color space of input. 'logc3' = ARRI LogC3 compressed HDR; 'linear' = linear HDR directly; 'srgb' = already-graded sRGB image (skips Reinhard tonemap).

output_tooltips: Tonemapped sRGB image, ready for preview/save.
HDRPreviewKJ.name: HDRPreviewKJ
HDRPreviewKJ.description:
Realtime-exposure preview for HDR-compressed images.

Input: LogC3-compressed [0,1] image/video batch (e.g. the VAE-decoded output of an HDR IC-LoRA workflow, prior to HDR decompression).

Decompression + exposure + saturation + Reinhard tonemap + sRGB runs in a WebGL fragment shader in the browser for realtime slider feedback, and the same math runs server-side to produce the baked sRGB IMAGE output. Slider changes update the preview immediately; the IMAGE output only updates when the workflow is re-queued.

model.tooltip: Model to attach the preview override to.

max_resolution.tooltip: Max preview side in pixels for the live widget. 0 = full sampler resolution (no downscale).

jpeg_quality.tooltip: JPEG quality for the live preview transport.

suppress_default_preview.tooltip: Suppress the standard sampler-node preview overlay while sampling, so only this node's frame updates. Progress bar still advances normally.

preview_frames.tooltip: Frames to sample from each video step's latent for animated preview. 1 = single frame (current behavior, fastest). >1 = animated WebP playing back at preview_fps. Only applies to video models (5D latents); ignored for image models.

preview_fps.tooltip: Playback FPS for the animated WebP preview. Ignored when preview_frames=1.

vae.tooltip: Optional LTX VAE for true-RGB previews. TAEHV-LTX = fast tiny decode (VAE pinned to GPU). Any other LTX VAE = full-quality decode via vae.decode() — MUCH slower per step.

ModelPreviewOverrideKJ.name: ModelPreviewOverrideKJ
ModelPreviewOverrideKJ.description: Adds a dedicated live-preview frame on this node, with overridable max resolution. Default ComfyUI preview caps at 512px; this node sends its own preview straight to a DOM widget on the node so pixel-space models (Chroma Radiance, ZImage, HiDream-O1, …) can be previewed at full sampler resolution.

model.tooltip: The model output by Model Preview Override (used to locate the captured frames).

after_sample.tooltip: Anything from after the sampler (LATENT or IMAGE). The value is ignored — it just forces this node to run after sampling.

GetPreviewOverrideFramesKJ.name: GetPreviewOverrideFramesKJ
GetPreviewOverrideFramesKJ.description: Returns the frames captured by Model Preview Override during the most recent sampling. Wire 'model' from Model Preview Override (the same one feeding the sampler) and 'after_sample' from after the sampler (LATENT/IMAGE) to enforce correct execution order.

input.tooltip: The image or mask to preview.

PreviewImageOrMask.name: PreviewImageOrMask
PreviewImageOrMask.description: Previews the input images or masks.

strength.tooltip: Sharpening multiplier. Values above 1.0 give aggressive sharpening.

radius.tooltip: Gaussian blur sigma for the unsharp mask. Larger = enhances coarser detail.

threshold.tooltip: Noise gate. Higher = only sharpen areas with more texture/detail. 0 = sharpen everything.

strength.tooltip: Blend factor for high-frequency detail. Values above 1.0 give a punchier effect.

radius.tooltip: Gaussian blur sigma defining the frequency cutoff. Larger = enhances coarser detail.

strength.tooltip: Blend between original (0) and fully deconvolved (1).

radius.tooltip: Sigma of the assumed Gaussian blur to reverse.

iterations.tooltip: Richardson-Lucy iterations. More = sharper but slower, diminishing returns past ~20.

ImageSharpenKJ.name: ImageSharpenKJ
ImageSharpenKJ.description:
GPU-accelerated image sharpening with multiple methods.

**RCAS** — AMD's Robust Contrast-Adaptive Sharpening (from FSR).
Single 5-tap cross filter that adapts to local contrast.
Minimal artifacts, good for general use with little tuning.

**Adaptive USM** — Unsharp mask with local variance modulation.
Sharpens detail-rich areas more, flat/noisy areas less.
More controllable than RCAS via radius and threshold parameters.

**High-Pass** — Extracts high-frequency detail and blends it back.
Gives a "clarity" enhancement feel. Uses radius to control detail scale.

**Deconvolution** — Richardson-Lucy iterative deconvolution.
Can recover actual lost detail from blur, not just enhance edges.
Uses radius as the estimated blur kernel and iterations to control convergence.

FilterZeroMasksAndCorrespondingImages.name: FilterZeroMasksAndCorrespondingImages
FilterZeroMasksAndCorrespondingImages.description:
Filter out all the empty (i.e. all zero) mask in masks  
Also filter out all the corresponding images in original_images by indexes if provide  
  
original_images (optional): If provided, need have same length as masks.

InsertImageBatchByIndexes.name: InsertImageBatchByIndexes
InsertImageBatchByIndexes.description:
This node is designed to be use with node FilterZeroMasksAndCorrespondingImages
It inserts the images_to_insert into images according to insert_indexes

Returns:
    images_after_insert: updated original images with origonal sequence order

SplitBboxes.name: SplitBboxes
SplitBboxes.description: Splits the specified bbox list at the given index into two lists.

BboxToInt.name: BboxToInt
BboxToInt.description: Returns selected index from bounding box list as integers.

BboxVisualize.name: BboxVisualize
BboxVisualize.description: Visualizes the specified bbox on the image.

GenerateNoise.name: GenerateNoise
GenerateNoise.description: Generates noise for injection or to be used as empty latents on samplers with add_noise off.

allowed_float_decimals.tooltip: Number of decimal places to display for float values

WidgetToString.name: WidgetToString
WidgetToString.description:
Selects a node and it's specified widget and outputs the value as a string.  
If no node id or title is provided it will use the 'any_input' link and use that node.  
To see node id's, enable "Node ID Badge Mode" in main settings.
Alternatively you can search with the node title. Node titles ONLY exist if they  
are manually edited!
'widget_name' can be a comma separated list.
The 'any_input' is required for making sure the node you want the value from exists in the workflow.

filename_prefix.tooltip: The prefix for the file to save. This may include formatting information such as %date:yyyy-MM-dd% or %Empty Latent Image.width% to include values from nodes.

output_folder.tooltip: The folder to save the images to.

file_extension.tooltip: The extension for the caption file.

SaveStringKJ.name: SaveStringKJ
SaveStringKJ.description: Saves the input string to your ComfyUI output directory.

A way to get previews in the UI without saving anything to disk.

GetLatentsFromBatchIndexed.name: GetLatentsFromBatchIndexed
GetLatentsFromBatchIndexed.description: Selects and returns the latents at the specified indices as an latent batch.

ScaleBatchPromptSchedule.name: ScaleBatchPromptSchedule
ScaleBatchPromptSchedule.description:

CameraPoseVisualizer.name: CameraPoseVisualizer
CameraPoseVisualizer.description:

JoinStringMulti.name: JoinStringMulti
JoinStringMulti.description:

with the **inputcount** and clicking update.

SimpleCalculatorKJ.name: SimpleCalculatorKJ
SimpleCalculatorKJ.description:
Calculator node that evaluates a mathematical expression using inputs a and b.

    Supported comparisons: ==, !=, <, <=, >, >=  
    Supported logic: and, or, not  
    Supported functions: abs(), round(), min(), max(), pow(), sqrt(), sin(), cos(), tan(), log(), log10(), exp(), floor(), ceil()  
    Supported constants: pi, euler, True, False

SplineEditor.name: SplineEditor
SplineEditor.description:
# WORK IN PROGRESS  
Do not count on this as part of your workflow yet,  
probably contains lots of bugs and stability is not  
guaranteed!!  
  
## Graphical editor to create values for various   
## schedules and/or mask batches.  

**Shift + click** to add control point at end.
**Ctrl + click** to add control point (subdivide) between two points.  
**Right click on a point** to delete it.    
Note that you can't delete from start/end.  
  
Right click on canvas for context menu:  
NEW!:
- Add new spline
    - Creates a new spline on same canvas, currently these paths are only outputed  
      as coordinates.
- Add single point
    - Creates a single point that only returns it's current position coords  
- Delete spline
    - Deletes the currently selected spline, you can select a spline by clicking on   
    it's path, or cycle through them with the 'Next spline' -option.  

These are purely visual options, doesn't affect the output:  
 - Toggle handles visibility
 - Display sample points: display the points to be returned.  

**points_to_sample** value sets the number of samples  
returned from the **drawn spline itself**, this is independent from the  
actual control points, so the interpolation type matters.  
sampling_method: 
 - time: samples along the time axis, used for schedules  
 - path: samples along the path itself, useful for coordinates  
 - controlpoints: samples only the control points themselves  

output types:
 - mask batch  
        example compatible nodes: anything that takes masks  
 - list of floats
        example compatible nodes: IPAdapter weights  
 - pandas series
        example compatible nodes: anything that takes Fizz'  
        nodes Batch Value Schedule  
 - torch tensor  
        example compatible nodes: unknown

CreateShapeImageOnPath.name: CreateShapeImageOnPath
CreateShapeImageOnPath.description:
Creates an image or batch of images with the specified shape.  
Locations are center locations.

CreateGradientFromCoords.description: Creates a gradient image from coordinates.

CutAndDragOnPath.description: Cuts the masked area from the image, and drags it along the path. If inpaint is enabled, and no bg_image is provided, the cut area is filled using cv2 TELEA algorithm.

GradientToFloat.description: Calculates list of floats from image.

WeightScheduleExtend.description: Extends, and converts if needed, different value lists/series

The batch size is determined by the length of the input float values.
Each mask is generated with the specified width and height.

FloatToSigmas.description: Creates a sigmas tensor from list of float values.

InterpolateCoords.description: Interpolates coordinates based on a curve.

PointsEditor.name: PointsEditor
PointsEditor.description:
# WORK IN PROGRESS
Do not count on this as part of your workflow yet,
probably contains lots of bugs and stability is not
guaranteed!!

## Graphical editor to create coordinates

**Shift + click** to add a positive (green) point.
**Shift + right click** to add a negative (red) point.
**Right click on a point** to delete it.
**Ctrl + click** to draw a bounding box.
**Drag bbox corners** to resize, **drag inside** to move.
**Right click on bbox** to delete it.

To add an image select the node and copy/paste or drag in the image.
Or from the bg_image input on queue (first frame of the batch).

**THE IMAGE IS SAVED TO THE NODE AND WORKFLOW METADATA**
you can clear the image from the context menu by right clicking on the canvas

SoundReactive.description:

Uses your browsers sound input options and requires.  
Meant to be used with realtime diffusion with autoqueue.

SV3D_BatchSchedule.name: SV3D_BatchSchedule
SV3D_BatchSchedule.description:

Note that SV3D is still a video model and the schedule needs to always go forward  
https://huggingface.co/stabilityai/sv3d

instruction_prompt.default: Expand the following prompt to add more detail

Superprompt.name: Superprompt
Superprompt.description:
# SuperPrompt
A T5 model fine-tuned on the SuperPrompt dataset for  
upsampling text prompts to more detailed descriptions.  
Meant to be used as a pre-generation step for text-to-image  
models that benefit from more detailed prompts.  
https://huggingface.co/roborovski/superprompt-v1

GLIGENTextBoxApplyBatchCoords.name: GLIGENTextBoxApplyBatchCoords
GLIGENTextBoxApplyBatchCoords.description:
This node allows scheduling GLIGEN text box positions in a batch,  
to be used with AnimateDiff-Evolved. Intended to pair with the  
Spline Editor -node.  

GLIGEN model can be downloaded through the Manage's "Install Models" menu.  
Or directly from here:  
https://huggingface.co/comfyanonymous/GLIGEN_pruned_safetensors/tree/main  
  
Inputs:  
- **latents** input is used to calculate batch size  
- **clip** is your standard text encoder, use same as for the main prompt  
- **gligen_textbox_model** connects to GLIGEN Loader  
- **coordinates** takes a json string of points, directly compatible  
with the spline editor node.
- **text** is the part of the prompt to set position for  
- **width** and **height** are the size of the GLIGEN bounding box  
  
Outputs:
- **conditioning** goes between to clip text encode and the sampler  
- **coord_preview** is an optional preview of the coordinates and  
bounding boxes.

Screencap_mss.name: Screencap_mss
Screencap_mss.description:
Captures an area specified by screen coordinates.  
Can be used for realtime diffusion with autoqueue.

ScreencapStream.name: ScreencapStream
ScreencapStream.description:
Captures a frame from a browser screen/window share stream.
Click 'Start capture' to select a screen or window to share.
Live preview is shown in the node. Works with auto-queue.

Crop controls:
- Drag on preview to draw a crop box
- Drag inside the box to move it
- Drag edges or corners to resize
- Shift+drag to lock aspect ratio
- Right-click or double-click to clear crop

WebcamCaptureCV2.description:

Can be used for realtime diffusion with autoqueue.
